baseline_retained.read: This is the retained TileLang tb256/gps512/cap320 candidate.

slotcache_probe.reason: Caching PairSlots in shared memory adds 64 int32 entries per CTA and pushes dynamic shared memory above the MI350 per-block limit.
slotcache_probe.read: The retained producer already sits exactly at the LDS limit, so a straightforward shared-memory route-slot cache is not viable at block_d64/gps512.

premasked_probe.read: Pre-zeroing invalid P/dP rows and removing the per-head PairSlots branch from P/dP staging is correct, but slower than the retained producer. The repeated route-index/control load is not the dominant wall.

reject: mfma_reduced_slotcache at block_d64/gps512 due LDS capacity
mfma_reduced_premasked at block_d64/gps512 due S4096 latency regression
decision.next_kernel_work: Profile or rewrite the TileLang producer body itself: reduce LDS bank conflicts and accumulator-table pressure, not only PairSlots loads. Candidate directions are narrower/striped group_acc layout, split-D or split-panel variants that preserve query-major MFMA reuse, or a different two-stage producer where the first stage emits fewer compact rows without the 512x64 FP32 LDS table.
